DECLARATION OF CHARLES E HILL IN SUPPORT OF PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION FILED BY DEFENDANT HILL, CHARLES E.

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CHARLES HILL'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED. THE ENTIRE CASE IS STAYED PENDING ARBITRATION WHICH CAN ADDRESS THE TIMELINESS OF NOTICE AND THE PROPERTY COVERED BY THE OPERATING AGREEMENT. IF THE ARBITRATION DOES NOT ADDRESS ALL PROPERTIES THEN THE LITIGATION MAY PROCEED AS TO ANY REMAINING PROPERTY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (301/PJB) JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Feb-25-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CHARLES HILL'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND TO STAY ACTION. Appearances: Chris Cole (978.9999) for plaintiff and responding party; Brett A. Pedersen of Bradley & Pedersen (434.3101) for defendant and moving party. Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The entire case is stayed pending arbitration which can address the timeliness of notice and the property covered by the operating agreement. If the arbitration does not address all properties then the litigation may proceed as to any remaining property. Order signed in open court. (301/PJB) Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600

Ntc Of Motion On Petition To Compel Arbitration And To Stay Action
Off Calendar Feb-04-2011 Continued To Feb-25-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
